rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW EBRAHIMOON VS. GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 7, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT BGSM, INC. DBA KELLER WILLIAMS PENINSULA ESTATES, STANLEY GUAN DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part; OVERRULED in part and moot in part. Demurrer to the 11th cause of action is moot in light of the ruling on the motion to strike. Demurrer to the causes of action for indemnity and contribution is sustained without leave to amend. Leave to amend was previously granted and cross-complaint failed to allege any facts establishing that cross-complainant and cross-defendant are joint tortfeasors as to the claims asserted/could be asserted by the Plaintiff. Demurrer is otherwise overruled - fraud, misrepresentation, UCL and Aiding and Abetting are sufficiently alleged. Declaration in support of opposition not considered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW EBRAHIMOON VS. GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 7, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT BGSM, INC. DBA KELLER WILLIAMS PENINSULA ESTATES, STANLEY GUAN MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT GRANTED. Eleventh cause of action included without leave of Court is stricken without prejudice for Cross-Complainant to seek leave to amend to include this cause of action. Prayer for referral to the Real Estate Commissioner is stricken. Opposition is devoid of any authority suggesting that such remedy is available. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JULIA LONG VS. LOUISE LONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 7, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT LOUISE LONG DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege fact is support of each element of each cause of action, including but not limited to facts to support "other factors" establishing existence of a fiduciary/confidential relationship going beyond mere relationship between parent and child and/or any other basis for legal and equitable claims. See Briggs v. Nilson (1964) 226 Cal.App.2d 342. The Court notes that courtesy copies of the moving papers and reply have not been delivered. Failure to comply with SFLR 2.7B in the future will result in the motion being taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
